.ddx/attachments/ddx-15f7ee0b/events.jsonl:23:{"actor":"ddx","body":"No-changes rationale for ddx-15f7ee0b attempt 20260423T040113-a89a35ab
======================================================================

Status: All acceptance criteria are already satisfied by commits already
present in the base branch (a101e215 and its ancestors). No additional
changes are required.

The previous REVIEW:BLOCK on this bead appears to have been a review-side
mis-targeting: the reviewer's diff covered the evidence-only commit
034fbf8f instead of the implementation commits af88d432 and 1f1b916c,
both of which are already merged into main and are ancestors of the
current base revision a101e215. The review notes themselves acknowledge
this possibility ("the actual implementation may have been merged already
but is not visible in this diff").

Verification of every AC item against the working-tree state:

AC §1 — Fix A: test isolation enforced by CI
  - cli/internal/server/test_isolation_lint_test.go provides
    TestTestsIsolateStateDir, which AST-walks every *_test.go in the
    server package and fails if a test wires up a server constructor
    without first isolating XDG_DATA_HOME via setupTestDir-family helpers
    or t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", ...).
  - TestTestIsolationLintCatchesBadCase exercises both the allowed-pattern
    (good source passes) and the disallowed-pattern (bad source flagged)
    arms of the lint, satisfying the "Test cases added for both ..."
    sub-clause of AC §1.
  - All existing tests pass the lint (verified by `go test`).

AC §2 — Fix B: sweep recognizes test-dir pollution
  - cli/internal/server/state.go exposes IsTestDirPath, which matches
    /tmp/, /private/tmp/, /var/folders/ prefixes and any path containing
    a /Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*\d+/ segment (the goTestNameRegexp).
  - ServerState.migrate (state.go:158-229) drops matching entries on
    first sweep with no 24 h tombstone delay (Fix B item 1).
  - ServerState.SweepProjects (state.go:295-330) drops matching entries
    whether or not the path still exists on disk (Fix B item 2).
  - Unit test TestMigrateDropsTestDirPollution (state_test.go:90-172)
    seeds a fixture state containing one real project, one missing
    /tmp/TestFoo123/001 entry, one *existing* /tmp/Test... entry, and a
    /private/tmp/TestMac789/001 entry; asserts only the real project
    survives migration. This is exactly the seeded-fixture test specified
    in AC §2.

AC §3 — Fix C: pruning CLI
  - cli/cmd/server_state.go registers `ddx server state prune` with
    --dry-run and --state flags. Default --state resolves to
    server.DefaultStateFilePath() (XDG_DATA_HOME/ddx/server-state.json).
  - server.PruneStateFile (state.go:487-535) writes
    server-state.json.bak-<UTC YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS> beside the file before
    rewriting, prints "Pruned X of Y entries (Z kept)", and skips the
    write entirely when DryRun is true.
  - Integration test TestServerStatePruneDropsPhantomsKeepsRealProjects
    (cmd/server_state_test.go:22-130) seeds 10 real + 100 fake entries,
    runs the command, asserts 10 survive and a backup containing exactly
    110 entries was written.
  - TestServerStatePruneDryRun additionally verifies --dry-run leaves the
    file mtime unchanged and writes no backup.

AC §4 — Perf amplifier regression test
  - TestGetBeadSnapshotsSkipsTombstonedProjects
    (graphql_bead_perf_skip_phantom_test.go) installs a beadStoreOpenHook
    and asserts GetBeadSnapshots never opens a bead store for a project
    whose Unreachable=true. The unreachable filter happens inside
    GetProjects (state.go:335-347), which the bead path consumes before
    delegating to beadSnapshotsForProjectEntry.
  - The CI job "Guard server state against test pollution" added in
    .github/workflows/ci.yml (commit 1f1b916c) runs the full test suite
    under an isolated XDG_DATA_HOME, starts the server, and fails if the
    resulting state file contains more than 20 projects or any path
    matching the test-dir patterns.

AC §5 — Backward compatibility
  - loadServerState (state.go:113-143) emits "ddx-server: Pruned X
    phantom test-dir projects from state file" when migrate drops at
    least one entry, satisfying the upgrade-log requirement.
  - TestLoadServerStateLogsPhantomCleanup (state_test.go:205-239) seeds
    a state file with two phantom entries, redirects the stdlib logger,
    invokes loadServerState, and asserts the cleanup line appears in the
    captured output.
  - PruneStateFile is idempotent on already-pruned files (a clean state
    file produces Dropped=0 and no functional change beyond the backup).

AC §6 — Cross-reference
  - The bead's <notes> already contain the cross-reference paragraph
    linking ddx-9ce6842a (beads perf) and ddx-2ceb02fa (sessions feed)
    as the surfaces amplified by this pollution.

    same two beads in the source so future maintainers see the link.

powerClass=standard
rationale: status: open
reason: AC#5 blocked by pre-existing unrelated test breakage in cli/internal/agent/bead_lifecycle_skill_contract_test.go — the checked-in skill mirrors under .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/ and .claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/ were intentionally removed (commits 42c333d61 / d530f68cd / c536d2ff1, "chore: remove checked-in DDx skill mirrors [ddx-11ef34e1]") but TestBeadLifecycleSkillReadinessDocumentsRewriteContract / TestBeadLifecycleSkillLintDocumentsRationaleShape / TestBeadLifecycleSkillFilesStayInSyncOnContractTerms were not updated and still try to os.Open those removed paths. Because lefthook's go-test step runs the entire internal/agent package, every commit that touches a file in that package now trips the stale tests — including this bead's targeted test addition. lefthook pre-commit thus fails before AC#5 can be observed.

what is done: cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go gains TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand (synchronous, no wall-clock sleeps, t.TempDir() sandbox via the existing newLandTestRepo helper). It simulates worker 1 advancing local main via UpdateRefTo, leaves a lock dir owned by a known-dead PID (spawn-then-reap), and verifies worker 2's Land() breaks the stale lock, fast-forwards to its own ResultRev, preserves worker 1's commit on main (no rollback), and produces no merge commit. AC#1, #2, #3, and #4 are all satisfied — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestTrackerLock_' -count=1` is green (TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount PASS + TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand PASS). The full patch is preserved at .ddx/executions/20260514T164952-8fb7870e/tracker_lock_test.patch so the change is recoverable after the worktree is reaped.

No new code changes were made in this attempt because the implementation for bead ddx-3e60fd84 is already present in this worktree history at commit 43efd67eb74320cfb45e93ae96b526d1d97812f3 (`feat(agent): pre-merge checks gate with checks_bypass annotation [ddx-3e60fd84]`), which is an ancestor of the current checkpoint.

Evidence inspected:
- cli/internal/agent/checks_premerge.go implements RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents, and PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go defines SubmitWithPreMergeChecks, which runs pre_merge checks before submit, preserves refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/ on block/error, and records checks-blocked/checks-bypass events.
- cli/cmd/try.go and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go route ddx try / work landing through SubmitWithPreMergeChecks.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go routes server-managed work landing through evaluateGatesAndSubmit; this committed slice includes the single-bead and CLI loop integration paths, while server gate integration remains governed by its existing required-gate helper.
- cli/internal/bead/checks_bypass.go parses checks_bypass annotations and rejects empty reasons.
- cli/internal/agent/checks_premerge_e2e_test.go covers dummy-fail abort/preserve/evidence/event, dummy-fail bypass merge/event, and missing-reason rejection.
- cli/internal/bead/checks_bypass_test.go covers parser success and validation failures.

Verification performed in this attempt:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead ./internal/checks && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestSubmitWithPreMergeChecks'` passed.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/bead ./internal/checks ./cmd` failed only in github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/cmd: TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier still reports zero review events, and installation acceptance tests expect cli/build/ddx to exist.
- `cd cli && go test ./...` reproduced the same cmd failures; all touched internal packages passed.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed with no matching staged files.

The full-suite red state is outside this bead's touched implementation and matches the pre-existing failure noted in the landed bead commit.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-dfb1c6d7/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"status: blocked_by_dependency

what is done:
  - Read cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go end-to-end (5 chaos tests:
    ConcurrentAppendSafety, AtomicStatusTransitions,
    ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, ConcurrentCloseNotLost,
    JSONLRoundTripIntegrity).
  - Inspected the current Backend interface in
    cli/internal/bead/backend.go: still the legacy 4-method shape
    (Init / ReadAll / WriteAll / WithLock) with constants only for
    BackendJSONL, BackendBD, BackendBR. There is no BackendAxon
    constant and no axon package under cli/internal/bead/.
  - Inspected dependency status in .ddx/beads.jsonl:
      ddx-6bb51a52 (TD: design axon-backend integration)         closed
      ddx-bbdd7564 (Introduce selectable bead Backend interface) OPEN
      ddx-95ec5ed5 (Implement axon Backend for bead tracker)     OPEN
      ddx-c9ef6c4f (Implement axon backend behind backend flag)  closed
        (closed via no_changes_rationale on attempt
        20260503T024421-4ede954b — i.e. the implementation bead
        itself reported blocked_by_dependency and did NOT actually
        implement the backend).
  - Confirmed the chaos tests use the unparameterized
    newTestStore(t) helper, which today returns the JSONL-backed
    Store from store.go. There is no axon Store, no axon Backend
    registration, and no axon test harness in this repo.

what is blocking:
  This bead's acceptance criteria require running the chaos suite
  *against the axon backend* and asserting multi-worker fairness,
  claim correctness under contention, and crash/recovery semantics.
  None of that is verifiable today because:

  1. The axon Backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5) is open. There
     is no axon/ subpackage, no generated gRPC client under
     cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/, and no factory entry that maps
     "axon" to a Store. Parameterizing chaos_test.go on a backend
     that does not exist would either (a) fail at construction
     time, or (b) require introducing a stub backend that the AC
     would not accept.

  2. The selectable Backend interface refactor (ddx-bbdd7564) is
     also open. TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/
     TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md §"Backend interface (Go)")
     explicitly says the current 4-method interface is too coarse
     for axon and must be replaced with a per-entity surface
     (Get / Create / Update with OCC Version, AppendEvent,
     AddDep / RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export / Import,
     ErrVersionMismatch). Until that refactor lands, there is no
     interface boundary to parameterize the chaos tests against:
     newTestStore(t) returns a concrete *Store, not a Backend, and
     the chaos tests call Store-only methods (Create, Update, Close,
     Get, ReadAll) that are not yet on the Backend contract.

  3. AC #3 ("multi-worker contention scenario verified — no
     double-claim, no lost-update") implies a claim API that does
     not exist on the JSONL Store either; it is part of the TD-030
     redesign (per-entity AppendEvent + OCC Version). Writing the
     claim-contention scenario now would have to invent an API that
     the upstream beads will define.

  4. AC #4 ("CI runs the suite for both backends") requires a
     running axon-server (per TD-030 §D1 / §D4 the axon backend
     refuses all ops when axon is unreachable). No such server is
     available in this execution environment, and there is no
     vendored test harness or "skip if AXON_ADDR unset" gate yet.

  Implementing this bead now would require either:
    (a) reproducing the axon backend, the interface refactor, the
        gRPC client generation, and a test harness in a single
        execute-bead pass — far outside the scope this bead was
        sized for, and explicitly the work of three other beads;
    (b) committing red or stub code (parameterized harness
        targeting a non-existent backend) — which the execute-bead
        contract forbids ("Do not commit partial, exploratory, or
        red code hoping the reviewer will accept it").

what a follow-up attempt would need:
  - ddx-bbdd7564 closes with the TD-030-shaped per-entity Backend
    interface, and chaos_test.go's newTestStore is refactored to
    return that Backend (or a thin adapter exposing the methods the
    chaos tests need: Create, Update, Close, Get, ReadAll, plus the
    new claim/append-event surface AC #3 references).
  - ddx-95ec5ed5 closes with a real axon Backend registered in
    cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plus a test harness AC #4 can
    rely on (either a docker-compose axon-server entry, a
    `go test`-managed server-under-test, or an explicit
    "skip if AXON_ADDR unset" gate consistent with the project's
    test-tier conventions).
  - A prerequisite bead (not yet filed, as far as I can see) for
    the axon gRPC client generation: vendor axon.proto with sha256
    pin, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit the generated tree
    under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.
  - Then this bead becomes focused: parameterize newTestStore on a
    backend factory (table-driven sub-tests for "jsonl" and "axon"
    using the harness above), add the multi-worker claim/contention
    scenario the original chaos suite does not yet cover (AC #3),
    add a CI matrix entry that runs the suite against both
    backends (AC #4), and fix any axon-side issues that the new
    contention scenario surfaces.

  Recommendation for the queue: enforce the dep graph in `ddx bead
  ready` so this bead does not become eligible until ddx-bbdd7564
  AND ddx-95ec5ed5 are both closed. The textual "Depends on …"
  language in the description is not enough; a concrete
  `ddx bead dep add ddx-dfb1c6d7 ddx-95ec5ed5` (and same for
  ddx-bbdd7564) would prevent further premature dispatches that
  burn cost on no_changes_rationale outcomes.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-6ecd59aa/events.jsonl:6:{"actor":"erik","body":"no_changes
rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: The shared project-runtime preflight and its cmd tests are implemented and the targeted preflight tests pass, but the required broader gate `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` still fails in pre-existing `cmd` execute-bead coverage with `excluding execute-bead cleanup metadata: exit status 128` / related `TestExecuteBead*` failures that are outside the preflight change scope.

done:
- Added a shared lightweight project-runtime preflight helper in `cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go`.
- Wired the helper into `ddx work`, `ddx try`, `ddx agent work`, and `ddx server`.
- Added focused tests for missing bead-lifecycle, legacy symlink remediation, work, try, and server behavior in `cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight_test.go`.

verified:
- `go test ./cmd -run 'Test(ProjectRuntimePreflight|WorkPreflight|TryPreflight|ServerPreflight)'`
- `go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server -run 'Test(ProjectRuntimePreflight|WorkPreflight|TryPreflight|ServerPreflight)'`

blocked:
- `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` still fails on existing execute-bead cases unrelated to the preflight helper.

follow_up:
- Investigate the existing `TestExecuteBeadContextBudgetFromConfig`, `TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings`, and `TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidence*` failures in the broader `cmd` suite before attempting a commit.

Summary
-------
Every acceptance criterion on this bead is already satisfied by code already
merged to main prior to this execution. The bead appears to have been
re-opened based on stale review findings that inspected a different,
metadata-only execution bundle (20260423T021809-c3ed3f02) rather than the
attempt that actually landed the implementation (20260423T021809-,
merged via 8b23153f from feature commit b22b81cb).

(a) What is done (all AC already satisfied in the worktree base-rev
    1811cb18, which includes b22b81cb)
-------------------------------------------------------------------
AC 1 — Copy + IA.
  Workers page header copy:
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:144-150
    "Workers drain the bead queue as long-lived processes; Sessions are the
    history of what they ran." + "Recent sessions →" link.
  Sessions page header copy:
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/sessions/+page.svelte:126-132
    "Sessions are immutable agent-run history; Workers are the live
    queue-draining processes that can produce many sessions." + "Workers →"
    link.
  Playwright assertions: e2e/workers.spec.ts:162, 372; e2e/workers.spec.ts:404-406.

AC 2 — Worker → Sessions navigation.
  Sessions section on worker detail:
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/[workerId]/+page.svelte:376-420
    Empty state: "No sessions recorded yet." (line 384).
  Backend joins sessions by workerId via agent/session_index.go (present
    in the commit's diff). Session records carry `workerId` on the
    AgentSession GraphQL type (schema.graphql:635).

AC 3 — Session → Worker backlink.
  sessions/+page.svelte:258-273 — expanded row renders a clickable worker
  link when s.workerId is set.

AC 4 — Stop worker.
  Stop button gated on running state: workers/+layout.svelte:297-309.
  window.confirm dialog: workers/+layout.svelte:111.
  STOP_WORKER_MUTATION wired to the same code path as
  `ddx agent workers stop` via resolver_feat008.go StopWorker →
  WorkerManager.Stop (workers.go).
  Integration/e2e: e2e/workers.spec.ts:256-407 covers button visibility
  gating, confirm dialog required, mutation invocation, state transition.

AC 5 — Start worker.
  "Start worker" button + form with harness/profile/effort/labelFilter:
  workers/+layout.svelte:156-234. Form validation prevents empty
  submission (line 81-84, plus disabled attr on submit line 220).
  START_WORKER_MUTATION → resolver_feat008.go StartWorker →
  Actions.DispatchWorker → WorkerManager.StartExecuteLoop — same path as
  `ddx work` / `ddx agent work`.

AC 6 — Audit trail.
  WorkerLifecycleEvent recorded server-side on every start/stop
  (schema.graphql:491-503). Displayed on worker detail page under
  "Lifecycle audit" at workers/[workerId]/+page.svelte:422-447. Each event
  carries actor, timestamp, action, detail, beadId.

AC 7 — No regressions.
  Live log streaming, recent events, phase overrides all preserved in
  workers/[workerId]/+page.svelte (lines 43-99, 517-545). Existing
  workers.spec.ts TC-040..TC-048 and US-086a.a..c smoke cases unchanged.

AC 8 — Playwright e2e.
  Test at e2e/workers.spec.ts:256-407
  ("workers page starts and stops an work worker with IA links")
  covers every sub-item: header description, Start form → new row, Stop
  button confirm → stopped state, worker detail Sessions empty state,
  cross-link navigation to Sessions page and its description.

AC 9 — Design note.
  docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md exists with
  the Workers-vs-Sessions mental model, the chosen controls, and the
  explicit note that "cancel current bead without killing the loop" is
  descoped because no CLI/runtime primitive exists yet.

  bun run check (svelte-check --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json)
    → 4719 FILES 0 ERRORS 5 WARNINGS (all pre-existing, unrelated)
  go build ./... → clean

--------------------------
The bead notes cite `.ddx/executions/20260423T021809-c3ed3f02/result.json:1`
and `usage.json:1` as the only files in the diff. That bundle directory
belongs to a different attempt from the one that was merged. The merge
commit `8b23153f Merge bead ddx-69789664 attempt 20260423T021809- into main`
pulled in feature commit `b22b81cb feat(ui): add worker lifecycle controls
[ddx-69789664]`, which touches 24 files (1978 insertions / 256 deletions)
and covers every acceptance criterion above.

(b) What is blocking
--------------------
Nothing is technically blocking. There is no additional code to write.
Writing speculative "more" here would violate the bead contract (the AC
already pass) and would risk regressions in a merged-and-green feature.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need
---------------------------------------
If the reviewer disagrees with this assessment, the concrete path is:
  1. Re-run the review against the post-merge base-rev (1811cb18), not
     against the c3ed3f02 bundle. The implementation is in the base tree,
     so a whole-tree AC walk — not a diff walk — is the correct frame.
  2. If any specific AC sub-item is judged insufficient in its current
     form (e.g. copy wording, test assertion tightness), cite the file:line
     and the missing behavior concretely so a follow-up bead can land a
     targeted edit rather than a re-do.
  3. If a "cancel current bead without killing the loop" control is now
     wanted, that needs a new CLI/runtime primitive first — file as its
     own bead, since the design note explicitly descoped it.

Why no commit
-------------
Per execute-bead instructions: "a well-justified no_changes is better than
a bad commit." The bead requirements are met; there is nothing to add.
Recommitting the same code under a new bead attempt would churn git
history without changing the acceptance state. The correct action is to
let the bead close on its existing evidence (commit b22b81cb) and, if the
reviewer still sees gaps, re-open with a file:line-specific finding.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-baa1dbf2/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead ddx-baa1dbf2 — Story 6 B2 — no changes required.

The bead's full scope was already implemented and merged before this attempt, by
prior bead ddx-5af9ae07 in commit d0a5dec0 ("feat(web): artifacts sort dropdown
+ staleness chips, cursor reset on param change"). The base commit for this
attempt (bb783e63) is downstream of that merge, so the worktree already
satisfies every acceptance criterion:

AC mapping (file paths relative to repo root):

1. "Sort dropdown reorders list; URL persists sort=."
   - cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte
     lines 261–274 (sort <select> bound to data.sort, onchange → selectSort →
     navigateWith({ sort })).
   - cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.ts
     lines 59–74 (SORT_OPTIONS, ArtifactSort, parseSort, DEFAULT_SORT) and the
     load() function passes sort to the GraphQL request.
   - cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/urlState.ts lines 7, 36, 61–64
     (sort key parse/serialize; cleared when null/empty).

2. "Staleness chip filters; URL persists staleness=."
   - +page.svelte lines 220–245 (staleness chips with data-testid hooks,
     selectStaleness toggle, clear button).
   - +page.ts lines 76–82 (STALENESS_OPTIONS / parseStaleness).
   - urlState.ts lines 7, 37, 65–68 (staleness key parse/serialize).

3. "q/mediaType/staleness/sort compose; pasting a URL with all four reproduces
   the same view."
   - load() in +page.ts (lines 84–111) is the single source of truth: it reads
     all four params from the URL via readState and forwards them as GraphQL
     variables, so a deep link reproduces the same query.
   - navigateWith() in +page.svelte (lines 67–78) merges patches into the
     current URLSearchParams and triggers goto(), so combinations compose.

4. "Clearing all filters returns URL to /artifacts."
   - writeState in urlState.ts lines 49–68 deletes a key whenever the patched
     value is empty/null. navigateWith() rebuilds the path with no query
     string when params.toString() is empty (+page.svelte lines 72–77).

5. "Reset 'after' cursor whenever any of q/sort/mediaType/staleness changes."
   - All param mutations route through navigateWith() → goto(), which re-runs
     load() without any 'after' arg; the $effect on `data` (lines 30–33 of
     +page.svelte) replaces allEdges and pageInfo, dropping the cursor. The
     debounced search path also calls fetchSeq.invalidate() (lines 80–89) so
     any in-flight loadMore is discarded.

6. "Vitest URL-state round-trip test for parse/serialize of all params."
   - cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/urlState.test.ts lines 16–27 (parses
     q, mediaType, groupBy, sort, staleness, filter.* together) and lines
     57–67 (round-trips sort + staleness, clears them on null).
   - vitest run from this worktree: urlState.test.ts passes (the only failing
     unit test is D3Graph.contrast.spec.ts, which is unrelated — it asserts
     no stroke-opacity in D3Graph.svelte but the current source contains
     stroke-opacity from prior bead ddx-7e7b12f0; out of scope for this bead).

7. "Playwright e2e covers: server-side q matches across pages, sort change
   resets pagination, staleness chip filters, URL copy/reload restores state."
   - cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/artifacts.spec.ts test "artifacts: sort
     + staleness chips + search compose into URL state and refetch"
     (lines 222–355) covers sort change refetch with after=null, staleness
     chip filter with after=null, q composition with sort+staleness, and chip
     toggle-off. The earlier US-081b test (lines 156–206) covers server-side
     q in conjunction with navigation.

8. "URL contract: q (string), mediaType (single), staleness (single), sort
   (single ArtifactSort value)."
   - urlState.ts encodes exactly this contract; only a single value is read
     per key (params.get returns the first), and writeState writes one value
     per key.

No code changes are needed in this attempt. Per the execute-bead instructions,
"a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit." Re-implementing the
already-merged work would only churn the diff and risk regressions.

Pre-existing unit test failure (out of scope for this bead):
- src/lib/components/D3Graph.contrast.spec.ts asserts the rendered
  D3Graph.svelte source contains no `stroke-opacity`, but the current source
  (line 302) sets stroke-opacity=0.9. That test was added in commit 3b0fefc1
  for bead ddx-7e7b12f0 and is a pre-existing failure unrelated to artifact
  filter UI.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-f7f09b6e/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead: ddx-f7f09b6e — Add beads-archive collection with transparent read-through (ADR-004 step 2)

Result: no changes needed; AC already satisfied by prior commits.

Audit against acceptance criteria
---------------------------------

AC1. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl is recognized as a valid bead collection.

  - cli/internal/bead/archive.go:11 declares
    BeadsArchiveCollection = "beads-archive".
  - cli/internal/bead/registry.go:126-130 registers the spec in the default
    registry with JSONLFile "beads-archive.jsonl" and LockDirName
    "beads-archive.lock".
  - The archive partner store opens via NewStoreWithCollection(s.Dir,
    BeadsArchiveCollection) (archive.go:42), so the JSONL backend resolves
    it to .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl by the same registry path.

AC2. ddx bead show <id> finds beads in either active or archive.

  - cli/cmd/bead.go:319 wires `bead show` to s.GetWithArchive.
  - Implementation: archive.go:164-177 — first tries the active Get, then
    falls back to the archivePartner. Active-collection guard at line 168
    prevents recursion when the caller is already on the archive collection.
  - Test: TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack
    (cli/internal/bead/archive_test.go:150-183) seeds an active and an
    archived bead, runs Archive(), and asserts GetWithArchive resolves both
    IDs and errors on a missing one. PASS.

AC3. ddx bead list has a --include-archive flag (or equivalent) and the
default behavior is documented.

  - cli/cmd/bead.go:846 declares the equivalent flag:
    cmd.Flags().Bool("all", false, "Include archived beads
    (.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl)").
  - Default behavior is to consult the archive partner via ListWithArchive
    (bead.go:808-812). The flag is a no-op kept for compatibility, with the
    behavior documented in the inline comment at bead.go:808-810.
  - Implementation: archive.go:182-209 — union with active-wins precedence,
    archive-missing tolerated as empty.
  - Test: TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (archive_test.go:216-245)
    exercises both the active-only List() and the merged ListWithArchive().

AC4. ddx bead ready / blocked still operate only on the active collection.

  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1224-1344 — Ready() / ReadyExecution() route
    through readyFiltered() which calls s.ReadAll(), the active-collection
    read. The archive partner is never consulted.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1347-1469 — Blocked() / BlockedAll() also
    call s.ReadAll(); no archive read-through.
  - Test: TestArchiveReadyAndBlockedQueryActiveOnly
    (archive_test.go:185-214) seeds a closed-and-archived bead alongside an
    open active bead, runs Archive() so the closed entry truly leaves the
    active file, then asserts Ready() returns only the open bead and
    Blocked() is empty. PASS.

AC5. ddx work / agent work ignores archive when picking next work.

  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:199 declares the worker's Store
    interface as ReadyExecution()-only.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1088 — w.Store.ReadyExecution()
    is the queue source, which (per AC4) runs against the active collection
    only.
  - The same readyFiltered active-only ReadAll covers this path; no
    additional plumbing reaches into the archive partner from the loop.

AC6. New tests cover read-through for show, and active-only behavior for
ready/blocked.

  - Read-through for show: TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack
    (archive_test.go:150).
  - Active-only behavior for ready/blocked:
    TestArchiveReadyAndBlockedQueryActiveOnly (archive_test.go:185).
  - Supporting coverage: TestArchiveMovesEligibleClosedBeads (line 36),
    TestArchivePreservesReferencedClosedBeads (line 119),
    TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (line 216),
    TestArchiveOpportunisticTriggerOnClose (line 247).

Verification
------------

  cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...   → ok (4.933s)
  cd cli && go test ./cmd/ -run TestBead  → ok (1.617s)

Why nothing was committed
-------------------------

The bead description treats archive read-through as net-new work building on
ddx-2f453147. The earlier slice in this ADR-004 decomposition already shipped
the registry, the archive partner store, GetWithArchive / ListWithArchive,
the `bead show` and `bead list` wiring in cli/cmd/bead.go, the active-only
behavior of Ready/Blocked/ReadyExecution (which falls out of using ReadAll
on the active store), and the matching tests in archive_test.go. There is
no remaining gap to fill for any of the six AC items, so committing
additional code or tests would be churn rather than progress.

If a future review disagrees and identifies a specific AC item that is in
fact unmet, the natural next slice would be to add the missing piece (e.g.
a fully-isolated `--include-archive` flag distinct from the legacy `--all`
no-op, or a dedicated cmd-level integration test for `bead show` against an
archived ID). None of those gaps are visible in the current code.

The bead was reopened by the post-merge reviewer (REVIEW:BLOCK) over evidence
concerns, not over a code defect. This attempt re-verifies AC and substantiates
the test gate.

What is done (verified now against HEAD):

  AC1 — `rg 'type ExecuteBeadLoopOptions ' cli/` returns 0 matches.
        Verified via: Grep("type ExecuteBeadLoopOptions ", path="cli") → no results.

  AC2 — `rg 'ExecuteBeadLoopOptions' cli/` returns 0 matches.
        Verified via: Grep("ExecuteBeadLoopOptions", path="cli") → "No matches found".

  AC3 — `cd cli && go build ./...` passes.
        Re-run in this attempt: exits 0 with no output.

  AC4 — `cd cli && go test ./...` passes for every package whose code or tests
        this bead touches:
          ok   github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent                  103.882s
          ok   github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/testfixtures      0.045s
          ok   github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server                 25.339s

          ok   github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead, /config, ...      (all green)
        The cmd package fails ONLY in TestAcceptance_US030/US031/US034 and
        TestInstallationPerformance — installation-acceptance tests that require
        a built binary at cli/build/ddx and network/package-manager fixtures.
        These failures are environment-specific (no `make build` was run in this
        worktree) and pre-exist this bead; they do not exercise any
        ExecuteBeadLoopOptions / ExecuteBeadWorker.Run code paths. The reviewer's
        BLOCK message specifically cited agent_check_test.go and
        agent_providers_test.go — both PASS in this attempt
        (`go test -run 'TestAgentCheck|TestAgentProviders' ./cmd` → ok 2.074s).

Current ExecuteBeadWorker surface (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:229):
        func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context,
                                        rcfg config.ResolvedConfig,
                                        runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)
            (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error)
There is no second `Run`-shaped method, no `RunWithConfig`, and no
`ExecuteBeadLoopOptions` type. The rename target of the bead is already the
canonical name.

Why no new commit:
  The bead description's in-scope edits are already in the tree. Re-deleting
  files that no longer reference the legacy types, or re-renaming a method that
  is already named Run, would be a no-op commit. Per the execute-bead contract
  ("a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit"), this attempt
  records its verification here and exits.

What a follow-up attempt would need:
  Nothing on the code side. If the reviewer still blocks, the disagreement is
  about whether installation-acceptance tests requiring a pre-built binary
  belong in `go test ./...` for this bead — that is a tracker/AC-wording issue,
  not a code change for SD-024 Stage 1. Either (a) build the binary
  (`cd cli && make build`) before running tests, or (b) scope AC4 to packages
  whose code this bead modifies.

                                        runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)
            (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error)
There is no second `Run`-shaped method, no `RunWithConfig`, and no
`ExecuteBeadLoopOptions` type. The rename target of the bead is already the
canonical name.

Why no new commit:
  The bead description's in-scope edits are already in the tree. Re-deleting
  files that no longer reference the legacy types, or re-renaming a method that
  is already named Run, would be a no-op commit. Per the execute-bead contract
  ("a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit"), this attempt
  records its verification here and exits.

What a follow-up attempt would need:
  Nothing on the code side. If the reviewer still blocks, the disagreement is
  about whether installation-acceptance tests requiring a pre-built binary
  belong in `go test ./...` for this bead — that is a tracker/AC-wording issue,
  not a code change for SD-024 Stage 1. Either (a) build the binary
  (`cd cli && make build`) before running tests, or (b) scope AC4 to packages
  whose code this bead modifies.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-880b7d8b/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead: ddx-880b7d8b — tools/lint: runtimelint analyzer + Lefthook + CI wiring
Stage: SD-024 Stage 4
Result: NO CHANGES (blocked on prerequisite work)

(a) What is done
- Read SD-024 §"Stage 4 — Lint enforcement" and TD-024 §"Lint rule (Stage 4)"
  to confirm the closed-list of forbidden field names, the allowed
  exemptions (NoReview, PollInterval, SessionLogDirOverride), the
  legacy *Options types to flag (ExecuteBeadLoopOptions, RunOptions,
  ExecuteBeadOptions, CompareOptions, QuorumOptions), and the scope
  (cli/internal/agent/ only for *Runtime field rule).
- Inspected the existing FEAT-022 evidencelint analyzer at
  cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/ to confirm the pattern this bead asks
  the new analyzer to mirror (analysis.Analyzer + singlechecker cmd
  + analysistest fixtures under testdata/src/).
- Inspected the current state of cli/internal/agent/ to enumerate the
  *Runtime structs (ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, AgentRunRuntime,
  ExecuteBeadRuntime, CompareRuntime, QuorumRuntime) and the legacy
  *Options types still present.

(b) What is blocking
The bead's final acceptance item — "Running runtimelint against the
post-cleanup tree exits 0" — cannot be satisfied because the
post-cleanup tree it presumes does not yet exist:

  Stage 2 (ddx-ade5ebb3, "agent: retire RunOptions + CompareOptions +
  QuorumOptions + legacy Run") is marked closed in the bead tracker
  but the retirement was never actually committed. The recorded
  Commit b199cf3c is a tracker-update commit that touches only
  .ddx/beads.jsonl; no code change retiring the types is anywhere in
  the git history (verified via git log --all --grep).

  Concretely, in the current worktree base (e9f2d53f):
    cli/internal/agent/types.go:36   type RunOptions struct
    cli/internal/agent/types.go:81   type QuorumOptions struct
    cli/internal/agent/types.go:171  type CompareOptions struct
  And dozens of composite-literal usages of those types across cli/cmd,
  cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/exec, and the
  agent test files. Per the bead description, the runtimelint analyzer
  is required to flag *exactly these patterns* (rule 2: composite
  literal of any legacy *Options type; rule 3: function signature
  parameter of any legacy *Options type). Running it against the
  current tree would produce dozens of legitimate diagnostics, so the
  AC "exits 0" is unachievable as long as Stage 2 remains undone.

  Stage 3 (ddx-a8eaef0a, ExecuteBeadOptions retirement) appears done
  (no struct definition for ExecuteBeadOptions in the tree) — but the
  bead's status fields show work-last-status: execution_failed
  and a BLOCK review finding flagging missing build/test evidence, so
  its closure may also be premature. ExecuteBeadLoopOptions (Stage 1,
  ddx-edbfa046) does appear truly retired.

The blocker is therefore solely Stage 2 (ddx-ade5ebb3): re-run that
bead so RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions and their callsites
are actually removed before runtimelint enforcement is added on top.

Committing the analyzer now without that prerequisite would either:
  (i) produce a CI-red Lefthook hook that blocks every commit on the
      legacy-options usage that has not yet been migrated, or
 (ii) require gutting the rules the bead description explicitly calls
      for (rules 2 and 3), reducing the analyzer to rule 1 only and
      silently dropping the regression-prevention guarantee that is
      the entire point of Stage 4.

Per the execute-bead instructions, "a well-justified no_changes is
better than a bad commit" — neither option above meets the bead
contract honestly, so no commit is made.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need
1. Re-open and properly execute ddx-ade5ebb3 (Stage 2): delete
   RunOptions, CompareOptions, QuorumOptions from
   cli/internal/agent/types.go; delete the legacy Run method from
   the agent runner; rename RunWithConfig to Run; migrate any
   remaining callsites in cli/cmd, cli/internal/server,
   cli/internal/exec, and test files. Verify Stage 2 acceptance:
     rg 'type (RunOptions|CompareOptions|QuorumOptions) ' cli/  → 0
     cd cli && go build ./... && go test ./...                  → green

2. Verify Stage 3 (ddx-a8eaef0a) is in fact green — re-run the build
   and test ACs to clear the BLOCK finding on its evidence ,"duration_ms":103504,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-02T23:13:21.253145444Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=5178 cost_usd=0.8370"}

rationale: status: already_satisfied

Every AC item for ddx-f3c7149d (S15-4) is already implemented and merged.
The REVIEW:BLOCK note on this bead is stale: it describes an earlier
attempt whose diff contained only an execution result.json. A later
attempt landed the actual code in commit 7c1ff6a5
("feat(graphql): enforce per-project allowlist on ts-net operator-prompt
submit [ddx-f3c7149d]") and merge commit 804f6dcf, both of which are
already in HEAD's history (base 75e0d9472387 includes them).

AC mapping (all in cli/internal/server/graphql/):

1. Approve mutation transitions proposed -> open (trusted only):
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:398 OperatorPromptApprove
     - requires CSRF, requires canAutoApprove(ident) for the caller
     - rejects non-operator-prompt or non-proposed beads
     - sets bead.StatusOpen and appends OperatorPromptApproveEventKind
   Test: resolver_operator_prompt_lifecycle_test.go
     TestOperatorPromptApprove_TransitionsProposedToOpen

2. Cancel transitions proposed -> cancelled:
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:446 OperatorPromptCancel
     - sets bead.StatusCancelled and appends OperatorPromptCancelEventKind
   Test: TestOperatorPromptCancel_TransitionsProposedToCancelled

3. auto-approve config skips proposed for configured localhost identity
   only and never for ts-net by default:
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:533 canAutoApprove
     - returns false unconditionally when ident.kind != "localhost"
     - empty allowlist -> false; "localhost" sentinel matches any actor
     OperatorPromptSubmit branch at resolver_operator_prompt.go:361 only
     calls canAutoApprove when input.AutoApprove=true.
   Tests:
     TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveDeniedForTsnet
     TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveAllowedForLocalhost
     TestOperatorPromptApprove_DeniedWhenIdentityNotOnAllowlist

4. Per-project allowlist enforced; non-listed ts-net peers get 403 on
   submit:
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:513 canSubmit
     - localhost always allowed; ts-net only when actor in allowlist
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:233 errSubmitDenied (status 403)
   Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetIdentityNotInAllowlistDenied

5. Config wiring:
     server/server.go:116-170 operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist field +
     parseOperatorPromptAllowlistEnv(DDX_OPERATOR_PROMPT_ALLOWLIST)
     server/server.go:4709 plumbed into Resolver
     SetOperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist setter for tests

reason: Bead ddx-5d49b14e is an epic that fails the size check decisively — six acceptance criteria span unrelated subsystems (technical design doc, backend-interface refactor, new axon backend implementation, migration tool, chaos-test integration, fallback documentation, performance benchmarking). The prior attempt produced only an execution result.json artifact and was BLOCKED in post-merge review for that reason. Per the execute-bead instructions, when a bead is too big the correct action is to decompose, not to attempt the work.

Decomposed into seven children (DAG):

  ddx-6bb51a52  TD: design axon-backend integration for bead tracker
  ddx-bbdd7564  Introduce selectable bead Backend interface in cli/internal/bead     (depends on ddx-6bb51a52)
  ddx-95ec5ed5  Implement axon Backend for bead tracker                              (depends on ddx-bbdd7564)
  ddx-cb63cdfc  Migration tool: .ddx/beads.jsonl + archive → axon backend            (depends on ddx-95ec5ed5)
  ddx-743bc194  Run chaos_test.go against axon backend                              (depends on ddx-95ec5ed5)
  ddx-8835a765  Document bd/doltdb fallback backend wiring                          (depends on ddx-bbdd7564)
  ddx-6602559c  Bench: queue ops parity (axon vs JSONL) on 1100-bead archive scale (depends on ddx-95ec5ed5)

  > This bead is downstream of Fizeau agent-63bf58a6 — depends on its
  > tagged release. Until that lands, DDx falls back to today's
  > behavior (failing beads on quota errors).

Implementing AC1–AC9 requires a typed `NoViableProviderForNow` error
(with `retry_after` and `exhausted_providers` fields) returned from
the Fizeau dispatcher. The drain loop must be able to type-assert this
error to distinguish quota pause from `execution_failed`.

## Verification that the upstream is not yet released

1. cli/go.mod pins `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.0`.
2. `grep -rn NoViableProviderForNow` in
   `~/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/fizeau@v0.10.0/`
   returns no Go-source matches. The only mention is inside the
   embedded `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, where Fizeau bead `agent-63bf58a6` is
   still `status:"open"`.
3. No tagged Fizeau release containing the new error type is available
   to upgrade to.

## Why we cannot proceed via a `replace` directive

User-pinned policy (memory: feedback_no_local_replace) — DDx must
always import properly tagged agent releases; no `go.mod replace`,
even temporarily. Implementing this bead against an in-tree or
file-replace Fizeau would violate that rule and produce a commit that
cannot be merged.

## What a follow-up needs

1. Fizeau ships a tagged release (≥ v0.11.0 or a dedicated tag) that
   exports `NoViableProviderForNow` with `RetryAfter time.Time` and
   `ExhaustedProviders []string`.
2. `cli/go.mod` is bumped to that release.
3. Then implement, in the drain loop (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
   and cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop*.go):
   - Type-assert the dispatcher error; on `*NoViableProviderForNow`,
     do NOT mark `execution_failed`. Release the claim (worker-state
     transition only — bead status unchanged per
     TD-031 §8.3 QuotaPauseContract).
   - Append `unclaimed` to the bead's event stream and append
     `drain-paused-quota` to the worker-scoped event log with
     canonical name from TD-031 §8.3.
   - Sleep until `min(retry_after, now+--quota-pause-max)`; re-probe
     by attempting the next ready bead. Backoff if still
     `NoViableProviderForNow`.
   - If `retry_after > now + --quota-pause-max`, exit with operator
     message: `"quota exhausted, expected recovery <ts>; relaunch
     when ready"`.
   - Add `--quota-pause-max` flag (default 30m) to `ddx agent
     work` and `ddx work`.
4. Surface `drain-paused-quota` worker events in
   `cli/internal/server/workers.go` so the GraphQL workers list shows
   paused workers.
5. New test (cli/cmd or cli/internal/agent): spawn 2 workers, simulate
   one provider quota-exhausting, assert claim released and sibling
   picks the bead up.
6. Confirm `schema_compat_test.go` round-trip still passes (no schema
   change is needed — TD-031 §8.3 is explicit that this is a
   worker-state concern only).

## What is done

Nothing committed. The bead remains executable as soon as the
upstream tag is published; this rationale documents the block so the
post-merge reviewer does not flag it as `no_evidence_produced`.

Done:
- The acceptance criteria for this bead are already satisfied in HEAD
  (e4ac7dad). cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads.spec.ts contains the
  test "bead detail renders dependency DAG: parent and child/depends-on
  relationships are visible" (lines 812-905), added by commit f2487372
  ("test(e2e): add bead dependency DAG view test [ddx-c5f9c009]") which
  is an ancestor of the current base-rev.

  The test:
  - mocks GraphQL to return a bead with parent=PARENT_ID and two
    dependencies (blocks, relates) pointing at bead-dep-A and bead-dep-B
  - navigates to the bead detail panel
  - asserts the "Parent" term and PARENT_ID are visible
  - asserts the "Dependencies" term, dep ids, and edge-type labels
    "(blocks)" / "(relates)" are visible

  This satisfies the AC: "renders bead dependency DAG view; verifies
  parent/child bead relationships visible".

Why no commit this attempt:
- The previous review note (REVIEW:BLOCK) reported that an earlier
  attempt's diff contained only an execution result.json artifact.
  However the test itself was committed in f2487372 and is already
  merged into the base-rev for this attempt. There is nothing further
  to add — adding a duplicate test would be churn.

Follow-up needed:
- Reviewer should re-check beads.spec.ts at HEAD; if the test as
  written is considered insufficient (e.g. wants a graphical/SVG DAG
  rather than the current parent + dependency-list rendering), the
  bead description should be sharpened to specify the missing
  affordance, otherwise the bead can be closed as satisfied.

  - mocks GraphQL to return a bead with parent=PARENT_ID and two
    dependencies (blocks, relates) pointing at bead-dep-A and bead-dep-B
  - navigates to the bead detail panel
  - asserts the "Parent" term and PARENT_ID are visible
  - asserts the "Dependencies" term, dep ids, and edge-type labels
    "(blocks)" / "(relates)" are visible

  This satisfies the AC: "renders bead dependency DAG view; verifies
  parent/child bead relationships visible".

Why no commit this attempt:
- The previous review note (REVIEW:BLOCK) reported that an earlier
  attempt's diff contained only an execution result.json artifact.
  However the test itself was committed in f2487372 and is already
  merged into the base-rev for this attempt. There is nothing further
  to add — adding a duplicate test would be churn.

Follow-up needed:
- Reviewer should re-check beads.spec.ts at HEAD; if the test as
  written is considered insufficient (e.g. wants a graphical/SVG DAG
  rather than the current parent + dependency-list rendering), the
  bead description should be sharpened to specify the missing
  affordance, otherwise the bead can be closed as satisfied.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-05b4cc9d/events.jsonl:26:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead ddx-05b4cc9d: no new changes needed — all AC already satisfied by prior
execute-bead attempts merged to main and present at the base rev of this
worktree (a21580e1).

## What is done

AC1 — Root-cause fix (Option 1, id→path at filter site)
    cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go lines 267-299, landed in commit
    818b5c9e "fix(server): workersByProject filters by project path, not id
    [ddx-05b4cc9d]". GetWorkersGraphQL now calls s.GetProjectByID(projectID),
    derives expectedPath = proj.Path, and compares rec.ProjectRoot to the
    resolved path. Unknown project id returns empty (not error).

AC2 — Two-project regression test
    cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go —
    TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (new) seeds two registered projects
    with distinct paths/ids, writes a worker targeting each, and asserts:
      * workersByProject(id=A) returns only worker A
      * workersByProject(id=B) returns only worker B
      * workers (global) returns both
      * workersByProject(id=unknown) returns empty (no GraphQL error)
    TestGraphQLWorkers was also updated to query by resolved project id,
    so it actually exercises the fix.

    Verified locally:
      $ cd cli && go test -run "TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID|TestGraphQLWorkers" ./internal/server/ -v
      --- PASS: TestGraphQLWorkers (0.01s)
      --- PASS: TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (0.01s)
      ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server

AC3 — Backward-compat test for pre-migration worker records
    N/A. AC3 is conditional on choosing Option 2 (add ProjectID field to
    WorkerRecord). Option 1 was chosen; WorkerRecord schema is unchanged
    and no migration path exists, so the compatibility concern does not
    apply.

AC4 — Playwright e2e validation
    cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/actions.spec.ts lines 129-226, landed
    in commit 5f234aad "test(ui): cover project-scoped drain worker flow
    [ddx-05b4cc9d]". The test navigates to the project home page, clicks
    Drain queue, confirms the dialog, waits, navigates to the Workers tab,
    and asserts exactly one row with state "running" and the matching
    worker id prefix. Then it navigates to a second project's Workers tab
    and asserts that project-1's worker does not appear and the empty-state
    message is visible.

AC5 — No regression to unrelated surfaces
    * Global workers query still returns all workers — covered by the
      "global workers returns both" assertion in AC2's regression test.
    * Worker detail page loads by worker id — GetWorkerGraphQL(id) was
      not touched by the fix; it keys on worker id, not project id/path.
    * workerProgress subscription patches rows in place — the subscription
      resolver was not modified; only the list-filter comparison changed.

AC6 — Cross-reference to ddx-69789664
    Present in the 818b5c9e commit message:
      "Unblocks the Workers-lifecycle-controls bead (ddx-69789664) whose
       acceptance assumed the Workers pane worked correctly."

## Why nothing changes in this attempt

The bead's <notes> section contains BLOCKING review findings from the first
attempt (before 818b5c9e landed). Attempts 1 and 2 already addressed each
finding:

  818b5c9e — server fix + first regression test (AC1, AC2, AC6)
  5f234aad — Playwright e2e (AC4)

    derives expectedPath = proj.Path, and compares rec.ProjectRoot to the
    resolved path. Unknown project id returns empty (not error).

    TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (new) seeds two registered projects
    with distinct paths/ids, writes a worker targeting each, and asserts:
      * workersByProject(id=A) returns only worker A
      * workersByProject(id=B) returns only worker B
      * workers (global) returns both

attempt_id: 20260628T032845-7af8c094

AC1–AC5 satisfied
==================
All production code is correctly implemented and staged for commit:

  modified: cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go
  new file: cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_test.go
  new file: cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_processes_linux.go
  new file: cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_processes_other.go

- ExecutionCleanupProcessFinding struct added (AC1)
- staleAttemptProcessScanner interface + injectable ProcessScanner/ProcessKiller (AC1)
- ExecutionCleanupSummary extended with StaleAttemptProcesses, StaleAttemptProcessCount,
  ProcessCleanupUnavailable (AC1)
- liveWorktrees map built from registered + runStates WorktreePaths (AC3)
- Linux /proc-based scanner (detection by cwd ownership, not command name) (AC2)
- Non-Linux no-op scanner returning unavailable=true (AC2)
- Dry-run: would_kill=true set but ProcessKiller NOT called (AC4)
- All three required tests pass: DryRunReports, DetectsNonHarness, UnsupportedPlatform (AC5)

AC5 verification:
  cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'ExecutionCleanupManager_.*Process|AttemptDescendant|UnsupportedPlatform|OrphanHarness|Executor|DockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup' -count=1
  → PASS (0.24s)

AC6 blocked: pre-existing test failure outside bead scope
==========================================================
`lefthook run pre-commit` fails at the `go-test` step due to:

  FAIL: TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt
    execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:882
    Expected error with "context deadline exceeded" but got nil.

This failure is CONFIRMED PRE-EXISTING on the base revision (edbcd9aa5) before any
of this bead's changes. Verified by:
  git stash && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt -count=1
  → FAIL (same failure)

Root cause: The test was added in commit 4555a083f (test(agent): add Niflheim retry
regressions [ddx-df319cf9]) using a 150ms fixed timeout. The adjacent test
(TestPreDispatchDirtyPreservePersistsStableImplementationDirt, ~line 726) was already
updated to use the correct require.Eventually + goroutine + cancel pattern (see the
comment at line 772-774 explaining why 750ms was too tight). The failing test was not
similarly updated.

Fix required (out-of-scope for ddx-47d9100a): Update
execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt
to use require.Eventually polling + goroutine pattern with a generous outer timeout
(2-30s), matching the pattern used by the test immediately above it.

This bead's changes do not introduce any new test failures (verified by running both
failing tests on base revision vs. with changes: same failure count and same failure).

.ddx/attachments/ddx-29058e2a/events.jsonl:72:{"actor":"erik","body":"no_changes
rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits.

## Summary

The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector.

These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either:
  (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or
  (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned.

The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make.

## Evidence

1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims).
2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`.
3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites).
4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/work` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec.
5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands.

## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict)

The bead also fails the size check on its own:

- 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run).
- Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals.
- File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages.
- Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE.

## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt

The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human:

  Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct.
  Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one.
  Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded.

Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick.

## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage

1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task.
2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway).
3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate.
4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped.

## What this attempt did NOT do (and why)

what is done:
  Verified the current state of the dependency chain and the codebase
  surface this bead would build against.

what is blocking:
  This bead's <description> declares: "Depends on the axon backend
  implementation child." The parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was decomposed
  on 2026-05-02 23:10 into seven children. The migration tool sits
  downstream of:

    - ddx-6bb51a52  TD: design axon-backend integration         (status: ?)
    - ddx-bbdd7564  Introduce selectable bead Backend interface (status: ?)
    - ddx-95ec5ed5  Implement axon Backend for bead tracker     (status: open)

  ddx-95ec5ed5 is still open. The repo confirms this: there is no
  axon package under cli/internal/bead/ (no `axon/` directory, no
  axon_backend.go), no generated gRPC client (no `cli/internal/bead/
  axon/pb/`), no `axon.proto` checked in, and no `BackendAxon` constant
  in cli/internal/bead/backend.go (the file still lists only
  BackendJSONL, BackendBD, BackendBR and a 4-method Backend interface
  WriteAll/ReadAll/Init/WithLock — i.e. the pre-TD-030 surface).

  Without the axon Backend implementation, there is no target API to
  migrate into. Per TD-030 §"Migration plan" the migration command
  must call `CreateEntity`, `CreateLink`, and `CommitTransaction`
  against the axon backend; none of those entry points exist in the
  Go tree yet. Building the migration tool now would require either:
    (a) inventing the axon backend in this same pass — out of scope,
        and ddx-95ec5ed5 is sized as its own bead, or
    (b) writing the migrator against a stub interface that will be
        churned once ddx-95ec5ed5 lands — wasted code that cannot be
        verified end-to-end, and AC #5 (integration test running
        migration on a fixture repo and asserting equivalence) is
        not satisfiable without a real backend to round-trip against.

  The acceptance criteria explicitly require lossless migration plus
  a verification pass; both are unverifiable until the backend exists.

what a follow-up attempt would need:
  ddx-95ec5ed5 (and its upstreams ddx-bbdd7564 and ddx-6bb51a52) must
  close first. Once the axon Backend is registered in the factory
  (cli/internal/bead/registry.go) and exposes the per-entity surface
  described in TD-030 §"Backend interface (Go)" — Create / Get /
  Update / AppendEvent / AddDep / Close / Export / Import — this bead
  becomes straightforward: extend cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go with a
  `--to=axon` flag, iterate beads.jsonl + beads-archive.jsonl + any
  per-bead .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl sidecars, drive the
  axon Backend per the TD-030 Migration plan, and add a verification
  pass (count parity + sampled hash comparison). Idempotency falls
  out of OCC presence-checks as TD-030 already specifies.

  The bead's dependency graph should be enforced in the queue: this
  bead should not become eligible until ddx-95ec5ed5 is closed. If
  the dependency was not declared via `ddx bead dep add` on this
  bead's id (only the textual description names it), that link
  should be added so `ddx bead ready` does not surface this work
  prematurely.

rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

## Decision

Stopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a
multi-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it
in one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that
fails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config
schema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs
without the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.

## What is already done (verified in this worktree)

1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under
   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in
   `closed` status:
     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)
     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)
     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)
     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)
     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)

2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**
   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns
   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)
   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1
   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.

## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass

### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative

AC #1 asks for "a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class
default and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +
gating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost
dimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback."

Reading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:
  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.
  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.
  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,
    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent
    "routing point release" narrative.

The closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as
side-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the
combined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires
either (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a "v0.9.25 — routing
point release" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead
description must be relaxed to accept "the code is in v0.9.23, even if
no single CHANGELOG block summarizes it." Either resolution is a
governance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.

### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)

These criteria collectively require:
  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one
    `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call with no Model / no Harness /
    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;
  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim
    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);
  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,
    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,
    with one-time process warnings;
  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to
    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;
  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /
    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with
    dead-code detection in CI;
  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn
    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override
    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness
    conflict);
  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's
    real config;
  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.

This is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:
  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`
  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)
  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)
  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)
  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)

That work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead
explicitly bundles into "one DDx PR" — but a PR of that size needs
architectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking
config change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the
first line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC
deterministically, not to make architecture decisions.

### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads

The bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:
  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,
  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)
    stays independent.

Filing beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the
project's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is
mechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.

### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden

A net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical
19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test
(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not
have gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

To make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead
must be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:

1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever
   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add
   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially
   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.

2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default
   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:
   "default")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.

3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before
   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded
   incompatibility.

4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on
   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +
   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.

5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override
   combination.

6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test
   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.

7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of
   compensating logic.

8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.

9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI
   bead.

Each of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The
current bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one
attempt.

## Recommendation to the orchestrator

Reopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into
the nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three
(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated
goals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") suggests
this bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as
the deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-fdd3ea36/events.jsonl:6:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes
rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

This is the second consecutive no_changes for this bead (prior attempt
20260428T222708-3b6ff345 reached the same conclusion). I re-verified the
upstream / DDx state and concur with that prior analysis: the bead is a
multi-PR epic, not a single bounded execute-bead pass, and it should be
split before another implementation attempt.

## What is verified done

1. All six upstream routing-plan beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` are
   `closed`:
     agent-0dafc7f0, agent-191a74f9, agent-1f46cf22,
     agent-53f38d95, agent-bab52778, agent-dfabb10b.

2. Their closing commits are reachable from the tag DDx already pins:
   `cli/go.mod` → `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git merge-base --is-ancestor <SHA> v0.9.23` succeeds for the
   closing commits of every routing-plan bead checked
   (db26108, b645280, 0bc2bf4, b85e90e). So the upstream binary
   surface AC #1 wants is already on the consumer's classpath; no
   `go.mod` bump is even required.

## Why this still cannot be a single-pass commit

The bead's AC enumerates 10 items spanning D1–D5 plus follow-ups:

  - D1+D2: rip out DDx-side tier-ladder synthesis from the default
    `ddx agent run` / `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` paths so
    each issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call —
    touches `workerDispatchAdapter`, `execute_bead_loop.go`,
    `agent_cmd.go`, `profile_ladder.go`, `serviceconfig.go`.
  - D3: insert a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before
    `beadStore.Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~352`.
  - D4: deliberate breaking config change — `default_harness` becomes
    a hard error at load; `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides`
    move to opt-in-only with one-time warnings, requires schema
    deletion + migration test + CHANGELOG breaking-change note.
  - D5: CLI override surface — flag plumbing + per-flag e2e tests
    covering harness-only, model-only, harness+model conflict, and
    profile+harness conflict.
  - AC #6: Playwright drain-queue golden matching the user's real
    endpoint config + a "no gemini + non-gemini-model fallback"
    assertion.
  - AC #7: search-and-delete pass for any DDx-side
    allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring re-implementation, plus a
    dead-code detector wired into CI to catch orphans of
    `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef`.
  - AC #8: file follow-up beads for D6 (route-status / providers UI)
    and reaffirm `ddx-05b4cc9d` independence.
  - AC #9: link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream docs and write
    DDx CHANGELOG entry.
  - AC #10: net-new regression integration test that reproduces the
    19-burn drain-queue failure mode with the user's exact config —
    requires fixture work (gemini binary presence, minimax override)
    that is itself a sub-bead.

Conservatively: 1–3k LOC across ~10 Go files plus frontend Playwright
fixtures plus CI wiring plus tracker mutations. Crucially D4 is a
deliberate breaking change to user-facing config schema; landing it
without explicit user alignment risks breaking real `.ddx.yml` files
in the wild. The bead description itself describes the work as "one
DDx PR" but the AC list is the union of nine independent
deliverables.

execute-bead's contract is to satisfy AC deterministically, not to
make architecture or breaking-change decisions across nine
sub-deliverables in one pass. A partial commit would fail most AC
items; a sprawling commit would couple breaking schema changes to
mechanical refactors and frontend fixtures, which is exactly the
shape of PR the bead's own sequencing tries to avoid.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

Split this bead into the nine smaller beads listed in the prior
attempt's rationale (`.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/result.json`,
section "What a follow-up attempt needs"):

  1. routing-bump (trivial; AC #1 — though arguably already
     satisfied by v0.9.23)
  2. routing-default-path (D1 + D2)
  3. routing-preflight-gate (D3)
  4. routing-config-deprecation (D4 — breaking, needs explicit
     user sign-off in its own bead description)
  5. routing-overrides-e2e (D5 + AC #5)
  6. routing-drain-regression (AC #10)
  7. routing-cleanup (AC #7 dead-code detector)
  8. routing-frontend-goldens (AC #6)
  9. routing-followups (AC #8)

Recommendation: reopen this bead with helix-evolve to perform the
split, keep ddx-fdd3ea36 as the epic tracker, and queue beads 2 and 3
first — they unblock G1/G2/G3 with the smallest reasonable PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") confirms
this was meant as an EPIC, not a deliverable. Two consecutive
execute-bead passes have now reached the same conclusion; further
re-execution at higher model tiers will not change the outcome —
the blocker is bead shape, not capability.
