rationale: Bead: ddx-fdd3ea36 — Adopt ddx-agent routing point release (DDx-side consumer)
Attempt: 20260429T003007-e136e75e (no-op)

(a) WHAT IS DONE
================
- AC #1 is satisfied by prior work on this branch:
  * cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent at v0.9.23 (verified).
  * CHANGELOG.md (commit 7d50a792) documents the consumed upstream
    capabilities and cites each upstream commit + bead. The changelog
    explicitly notes that upstream shipped routing capabilities incrementally
    across v0.9.10..v0.9.24 rather than as a single tag.
  * All six upstream beads labeled `routing-plan` in
    ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl are CLOSED:
      agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware tiebreak)
      agent-191a74f9 (first-class default + local + standard profiles)
      agent-1f46cf22 (routing reference docs)
      agent-53f38d95 (public RouteDecision.Candidates trace)
      agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
      agent-dfabb10b (typed ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / ErrProfilePinConflict)
  * The needed public types are present in the pinned module at
    /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/agent@v0.9.23/routing_errors.go:
      ErrHarnessModelIncompatible, ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate,
      ErrUnknownProfile, ErrNoLiveProvider, DecisionWithCandidates interface
      with RouteCandidates() []RouteCandidate.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING THIS ATTEMPT
=================================
The bead is an epic-tracker, not a single deliverable. AC #2 through #10
collectively require — in one PR with no compensating paths retained — all of:

  D1. workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker rewrite + Drain-button parity.
  D2. Removal of default-path tier-ladder synthesis from
      ddx agent run / ddx agent execute-bead / ddx work; ResolveProfileLadder
      and ResolveTierModelRef gated behind --escalate only.
  D3. Preflight gate inserted before beadStore.Claim in execute_bead_loop.go
      (the bead points at line 352, which has already drifted — the actual
      Claim site is now line 341; the loop is 1052 lines and has substantial
      surrounding dispatch state to thread through).
  D4. Hard-error breaking change for `agent.routing.default_harness`;
      one-time warnings + default-path ignore for `profile_ladders` and
      `model_overrides`. Schema deletion in
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json. Config-load test fixtures.
  D5. CLI surface parity for ddx agent run (no flags / --profile / --harness /
      --model / combos), ddx work, ddx agent execute-bead.

  Plus tests demanded by the AC:
  - AC #2 integration test reproducing the original 19-burn drain failure
    against the user's real config shape.
  - AC #3 worker-level failure record assertion with seeded pathological route
    via an upstream test seam that does not currently exist on the DDx side.
  - AC #5 four end-to-end override-behavior tests (incl. cost-tiebreak
    seeded-providers test).
  - AC #6 Playwright golden for the drain-queue UI path
    (cli/internal/server/frontend); fixture must mirror user's real
    endpoint config.
  - AC #7 dead-code detector wired into CI catching orphaned ladder helpers.
  - AC #10 regression-guard integration test that fails before this work and
    succeeds after.

  Plus the directive in AC #7 — "Search DDx for code that re-implements any
  of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping,
  provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review." —
  requires a deliberate audit + removal pass spanning at least:
      cli/internal/agent/harness_config.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/config.go
      cli/internal/config/types.go
      cli/internal/config/routing_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json
      cli/internal/server/workers.go
      cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go
      cli/cmd/agent_run_profile_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (1052 lines)
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_runwithconfig_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resolvers_e2e_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_pin_propagation_test.go

This is a multi-thousand-line, multi-subsystem refactor with a hard
breaking-change config migration and a new Playwright golden. It cannot be
landed responsibly in a single execute-bead pass without a much narrower
sub-bead breakdown. Attempting it as one commit risks: half-finished tier-
ladder removal that leaves the default path in an undefined state; a config
schema break that hard-errors existing user configs without the migration
warnings actually firing; or merging a Playwright golden whose fixture
silently passes because the underlying dispatch path was not fully rewired.

The previous attempt already correctly scoped this bead to AC #1 only and
documented the upstream prerequisite. Doing more here without
decomposition would either produce a bad commit or violate
DOWITYTD on a bead whose own description acknowledges it is a
"DDx-side tracker only" for an upstream-driven epic.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT NEEDS
==================================
Decompose ddx-fdd3ea36 into independently shippable sub-beads, each
small enough to land in one execute-bead pass with focused tests:

  ddx-<new>-D1: workerDispatchAdapter + Drain button — empty-spec defaults to
                Profile:"default"; no other synthesis. Unit test +
                dashboard wiring. Closes AC #2 partially.

  ddx-<new>-D2a: Default path stops calling ResolveTierModelRef /
                 ResolveProfileLadder. Add --escalate flag that re-enables
                 them. Tests assert call graph. Closes AC #2 fully.

  ddx-<new>-D3: Preflight gate before Claim using upstream's typed
                ErrHarnessModelIncompatible. Targeted test using upstream
                test seam (file the upstream test-seam ask as a separate
                ddx-agent bead if missing). Closes AC #3.

  ddx-<new>-D4: Config breaking-change migration. Hard-error on
                default_harness; warn-and-ignore on profile_ladders/
                model_overrides; schema update; migration fixture tests;
                CHANGELOG migration guide. Closes AC #4.

  ddx-<new>-D5: CLI flag-surface parity tests for ddx agent run / work /
                execute-bead with the four override permutations from AC #5.
                Closes AC #5.

  ddx-<new>-Audit: Audit pass for AC #7 (compensating logic removal +
                   dead-code orphan detector in CI). Closes AC #7.

  ddx-<new>-Regression: AC #10 regression-guard integration test that
                        reproduces the 19-burn drain bug; AC #2's
                        integration sibling. Closes AC #10 (and validates
                        D1+D2+D3 together).

  ddx-<new>-Playwright: AC #6 frontend golden against the rewired Drain
                        path. Last in sequence — depends on D1+D2+D3 +
                        Regression. Closes AC #6.

  ddx-<new>-Docs: Cross-link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream profile
                  catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract
                  docs (AC #9 second half — CHANGELOG half is already in
                  CHANGELOG.md from the prior commit).

  ddx-<new>-D6-followup: Decision-trace consumers in route-status /
                         providers UI — explicitly out of scope per the
                         bead description but should be filed now so the
                         tracking exists (bead description's own AC #8
                         requires this).

ddx-fdd3ea36 should remain open as the umbrella tracker until all
sub-beads close, at which point AC #2..#10 can be re-verified end-to-end
in one final review pass.

NO CODE CHANGES IN THIS ATTEMPT.

rationale: Bead: ddx-fdd3ea36 — Adopt ddx-agent routing point release (DDx-side consumer epic)

Disposition: no_changes — this is an epic/tracker bead whose work has been
deliberately decomposed into nine child beads. Some are closed; four remain
open. The tracker cannot close until those four close. There is no
implementation work that belongs at the epic level itself.

================================================================================
What is done
================================================================================

Upstream prerequisite (AC #1):
  All six routing-plan beads in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/ are closed and merged
  into tagged ddx-agent releases (v0.9.10..v0.9.24):
    agent-191a74f9  profiles (default/local/standard)         closed
    agent-bab52778  supported-models allow-list + gating      closed
    agent-dfabb10b  ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / PinConflict closed
    agent-53f38d95  public RouteDecision.Candidates           closed
    agent-0dafc7f0  CostUSDPer1kTokens + cost tiebreak        closed
    agent-1f46cf22  routing documentation                     closed
  cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23, which contains
  every routing-plan commit (verified via `git tag --contains <sha>` against
  closing_commit_sha for each upstream bead).

DDx-side child beads already closed:
  ddx-967e9802  routing-bump (go.mod pin + CHANGELOG)         closed
  ddx-755f5881  routing-default-path (D1 + D2)                closed   → AC #2
  ddx-98e6e9ef  routing-preflight-gate (D3)                   closed   → AC #3
  ddx-dd4c423d  routing-drain-regression test                 closed   → AC #10

Code evidence the closed work is in tree:
  cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go:20-21
    test seams verify ResolveProfileLadder/ResolveTierModelRef are NOT called
    on the default path (per ddx-755f5881 AC #3)
  cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:84-120
    DispatchWorker normalizes empty input to {Profile: "default"} only
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:360-392
    routing preflight gate runs BEFORE Claim at line 394 (D3, ddx-98e6e9ef)
  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1683-1684
    --escalate / --override-model flags exist; tier ladder + model_overrides
    are off by default (consistent with D2/D4 design)
  cli/internal/config/types.go:230-234
    migration note: agent.routing.default_harness was REMOVED in ddx-87fb72c2

================================================================================
What is open (blocks tracker close)
================================================================================

ddx-87fb72c2  routing-config-deprecation                      open  → AC #4
              hard error on default_harness; ladders/overrides opt-in only;
              CHANGELOG migration entry. (Note: the field-removal half appears
              landed per types.go:230-234 — verify what residual scope this
              bead still owns before executing it.)

ddx-2589e256  routing-overrides-e2e                           open  → AC #5
              --harness/--model/--profile flag plumbing parity tests for
              ResolveRoute(Profile, Harness, Model) shape; cost-tiebreak e2e;
              typed-error surfacing for incompatible combos.

ddx-950321ef  routing-frontend-goldens                        open  → AC #6
              Playwright drain-queue fixtures matching the real failing
              config; Drain button → live-endpoint-backed harness;
              never gemini+non-gemini-model; never 19-burn.

ddx-653f6ac9  routing-cleanup                                 open  → AC #7
              dead-code detector for orphaned compensating logic;
              remove residual allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring code
              that re-implements upstream behavior.

ddx-a0cf398b  routing-followups                               open  → AC #8
              file the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer
              bead; close out D6/D7 housekeeping.

================================================================================
Why this bead does not get implementation in this pass
================================================================================

1. The epic's AC is the union of its children's AC. Every remaining AC item
   (#4, #5, #6, #7, #8, plus the documentation half of #9) is the contract of
   a specific open child bead. Implementing them here would either duplicate
   work that the child bead will redo, or steal scope that another worker
   may already be planning to claim.

2. The bead description's "Sequencing" section explicitly orders:
     1. upstream lands (done)
     2. go.mod bump (done — ddx-967e9802)
     3. D1+D2+D3+D5 land together (D1+D2=ddx-755f5881 done; D3=ddx-98e6e9ef
        done; D5=ddx-2589e256 OPEN)
     4. D4 (ddx-87fb72c2) lands with or right after the above (OPEN)
     5. follow-ups (ddx-a0cf398b, ddx-950321ef, ddx-653f6ac9) (OPEN)
   The tracker is meant to close once steps 3–5 complete via their child
   beads, not to carry independent code.

3. Attempting any of {D4, override e2e, playwright goldens, dead-code sweep}
   in this single execute-bead pass would land a sprawling cross-cutting
   commit that violates the per-child-bead acceptance contracts and is
   hard to review. Per CLAUDE.md "DOWITYTD" and the merge-policy memory,
   the right action is to execute the children individually.

================================================================================
What a follow-up attempt should do
================================================================================

This tracker should NOT be re-queued for direct execution. Instead:

  a) Execute the four open child beads in their natural order:
        ddx-87fb72c2 (D4 — config deprecation residue + CHANGELOG)
        ddx-2589e256 (D5 — override flag e2e)
        ddx-950321ef (D6-equivalent — Playwright goldens)
        ddx-653f6ac9 (cleanup / dead-code sweep)
        ddx-a0cf398b (file follow-up beads)
     Each closes one or two AC items on this tracker.

  b) When all five close, re-open this tracker briefly to:
        - verify every AC item maps to a closed child or in-tree evidence
        - update DDx CHANGELOG (AC #9) if not already done by ddx-967e9802
        - close ddx-fdd3ea36 with a closing-comment that points at each
          child bead's closing commit.

That close step is documentation/bookkeeping — a single small commit, well
suited to a future execute-bead pass once the children are green.

No code changes were made in this pass. Workspace is clean.

rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: This bead requires benchmarking the axon backend against the JSONL backend on a 1100-bead corpus, but the axon backend has not yet been implemented. The implementing bead, ddx-95ec5ed5 ("Implement axon Backend for bead tracker"), is still status=open, and there is no axon_backend.go (or equivalent) in cli/internal/bead/ — the only Backend implementation present is backend_jsonl.go. AC2 ("Runs against both jsonl and axon backends") and AC3 ("Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl") are unsatisfiable without that backend.

The bead's dependency DAG was not encoded: per the parent epic (ddx-5d49b14e) decomposition, this benchmark bead was created with an implicit dependency on ddx-95ec5ed5, but no `ddx bead dep add` edge was recorded, so the queue surfaced this bead as ready prematurely.

Recommended follow-up:
  1. ddx bead dep add ddx-781a15cf ddx-95ec5ed5
     (record the missing edge so the queue stops surfacing this bead until the axon backend lands)
  2. Land ddx-bbdd7564 (Backend interface refactor, in_progress) and ddx-95ec5ed5 (axon implementation) first.
  3. Re-queue ddx-781a15cf; at that point the benchmark can be written meaningfully against both backends.

Writing a partial benchmark (JSONL only) would satisfy neither AC2 nor AC3 and would mislead the parent epic's AC6 ("parity or better than JSONL") gate.

The remaining live files in scope are `cli/internal/agent/routing_metrics.go` and `cli/internal/agent/model_catalog_yaml.go`, but they do not define the deleted functions named in the bead. `go build ./...` completes, but `go test ./...` still fails in unrelated `cli/cmd` installation acceptance tests because `cli/build/ddx` is an ar archive, so the suite cannot execute the expected binary. There is no additional source deletion to make in this snapshot without changing build plumbing outside the bead scope.

- The FEAT-019 contraction described in docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md

- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md now scopes FEAT-019 to the evaluation UX
  layer: comparison record storage and display (req 1–6), grading rubric storage/display (req 7–10),
  grading invocation (req 11–16), benchmark result aggregation (req 17–19a), bead-session linkage
  (req 20–21), and replay result display (req 22–25).

  against the spec; producing an empty or cosmetic commit would not satisfy any AC item and would
  pollute history.

  (commit 2950c2ca) rather than against the prior attempt's empty diff. The contraction is in place.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-c9ef6c4f/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"status: needs_investigation
reason: |
  Bead ddx-c9ef6c4f ("Implement axon backend behind selectable backend flag")
  cannot be completed in a single execute-bead pass because two upstream
  prerequisites are not yet in place, and the AC as written requires a
  working end-to-end integration against an external Rust service. Forcing
  a commit here would land non-functional scaffolding (the failure mode
  the parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was already BLOCKED for).

  Specific gaps observed in the current worktree (base ab0a056a):

  1. Dependency bead ddx-bbdd7564 ("Introduce selectable bead Backend
     interface in cli/internal/bead") is still status=open. The current
     cli/internal/bead/backend.go defines only the legacy interface:

         Backend interface {
             Init() error
             ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
             WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
             WithLock(fn func() error) error
         }

     TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md
     §"Backend interface (Go)") explicitly says this interface is "too
     coarse for Axon — WriteAll(beads []Bead) cannot reasonably be
     implemented against a remote entity store" and prescribes a richer
     per-entity surface (Get/Create/Update with OCC Version,
     AppendEvent, AddDep/RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export/Import,
     ErrVersionMismatch, etc.). Implementing the axon backend against
     the legacy 4-method interface would (a) violate TD-030 and (b)
     produce a backend that cannot honor claim/OCC semantics that the
     bead description and AC #3 require ("plus claim semantics and
     event append"). This bead's <description> says it "follows the TD
     authored in the design child" — that TD assumes the interface
     refactor has landed first.

  2. No Axon Go SDK or generated gRPC client exists in this repo.
     /home/erik/Projects/axon ships axon.proto at
     crates/axon-server/proto/axon.proto (377 lines, AxonService with
     CreateEntity/GetEntity/UpdateEntity/CommitTransaction/Traverse/
     QueryEntities/PutSchema/CreateCollection/etc.) but no Go bindings.
     TD-030 §"Wire transport" specifies generation via
     protoc-gen-go + protoc-gen-go-grpc into cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/
     with a `make gen-axon-client` target and a checked-in axon.proto
     plus sha256. None of that scaffolding exists; protoc toolchain
     setup, proto vendoring, and the make target are themselves a
     non-trivial chunk of work that should be its own bead.

  3. AC #5 ("ddx bead commands work end-to-end against the axon
     backend on a fresh repo, no migration required") cannot be
     verified inside this worktree. Per TD-030 §D1 the operator runs a
     separate axon-server process; per §D4 the backend refuses all ops
     when Axon is unreachable. There is no Axon server running in this
     execution environment and no fixture / test-double that the AC
     would accept ("end-to-end ... on a fresh repo" rules out an
     in-memory fake).

  Additional context: the parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was BLOCKED in
  post-merge review on 2026-05-02 for landing only execution evidence
  with no implementation, then decomposed into seven children. This
  bead is one of those children but was queued out of dependency order
  — ddx-bbdd7564 (the interface refactor child) needs to land first.

what_is_done:
  - Read TD-030 (the design child's output) end-to-end.
  - Confirmed current Backend interface in cli/internal/bead/backend.go
    is the legacy form, not the TD-030 form.
  - Confirmed dependency bead ddx-bbdd7564 is still open.
  - Confirmed no axon proto/client is vendored under cli/internal/bead/.

what_is_blocking:
  - ddx-bbdd7564 must land first to introduce the per-entity Backend
    interface (Get/Create/Update with Version/OCC, AppendEvent,
    AddDep/RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export/Import, error sentinels)
    that this bead's axon implementation needs to satisfy.
  - A separate bead should establish the gRPC client generation
    pipeline: vendor cli/internal/bead/axon/proto/axon.proto from the
    axon repo (with sha256 pin), add the make gen-axon-client target,
    add tooling docs for protoc-gen-go / protoc-gen-go-grpc, and
    commit the generated cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/ tree. This is
    explicitly called out by TD-030 §"Wire transport" but is not in
    the current bead's scope and has no existing bead covering it.
  - AC #5 needs a clarified verification path: either (a) require a
    running Axon test server in CI (with the deployment work that
    implies), or (b) loosen "end-to-end" to mean "round-trip against
    a vendored Axon test harness" and add the harness as a bead.

what_a_followup_would_need:
  - Re-queue this bead (ddx-c9ef6c4f) only after ddx-bbdd7564 closes
    with the TD-030-shaped Backend interface in place.
  - Add (and close) a new prerequisite bead for the axon gRPC client
    generation: vendor proto, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit
    generated stubs under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.
  - Add a new prerequisite bead for the Axon test harness used by AC
    #5 (either a test-only docker-compose entry, a `go test`-managed
    server-under-test, or an explicit "skip if AXON_ADDR unset"
    integration-test gate per the project's existing test-tier
    conventions).
  - Then this bead becomes a focused implementation: wrap the
    generated client in cli/internal/bead/axon/, register the
    "axon" backend in cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plumb the
    DDX_BEAD_BACKEND / .ddx/config.yaml selector, and add the
    create/claim/close/dep unit tests AC #4 names (against the
    harness from the new prerequisite bead).
rationale: status: needs_investigation

.ddx/attachments/ddx-88e91fdb/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead: ddx-88e91fdb — Add chat interface to project home page

Disposition: no commit this pass; recommend decomposing into 4–5 smaller beads.

(a) What is done in this pass
- Read the bead description and 12 acceptance criteria.
- Mapped the existing surface area:
  - GraphQL schema at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql with gqlgen-generated resolvers; subscription pattern established in resolver_sub_worker.go / resolver_sub_bead.go / resolver_sub_exec.go (buffered channel + ctx-cancel goroutine).
  - Frontend GraphQL via graphql-request (queries) + graphql-ws (subscriptions) — see cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/gql/{client.ts,subscriptions.ts}. No Houdini.
  - Project home page at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte (queue summary + 3 action cards). This is the host surface for the chat pane.
  - Sessions index at cli/internal/agent/session_index.go, sharded JSONL under .ddx/sessions/ (monthly). agentSessions GraphQL query already exists. This is the linkage target for AC5.
  - Agent execution entry at cli/internal/agent/service_run.go (RunWithConfigViaService → executeOnService → records a session-index row). Streaming today is per-harness; the work streams events via worker progress, not per-prompt chunks.
  - Playwright e2e pattern in cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/**/*.e2e.ts uses page.route('/graphql') interception with fixture data; no live backend.

(b) What is blocking a one-pass implementation
The bead bundles a feature that fans out across at least 5 independently-reviewable surfaces:

  1. Storage layer (.ddx/chat/<thread-id>/{manifest.json,messages.jsonl}) — new Go package with tests, append-only invariants, project/operator scoping.
  2. GraphQL schema + resolvers — chatThreads/chatThread/chatMessages queries, chatThreadCreate/chatSend mutations, chatStream subscription. Requires schema.graphql edits, gqlgen regeneration of generated.go, models.go updates, four new resolver files, integration tests.
  3. service.Execute integration — chatSend must dispatch a session and stream chunks + tool calls back over the chatStream subscription. Today service.Execute is request/response with a non-streaming Result; per-chunk streaming over the subscription bus is new infrastructure. Either: (i) add a streaming variant of executeOnService, or (ii) tail the session's evidence stream and re-emit. Both have non-trivial design choices that belong in the design note before code lands.
  4. Frontend — chat pane component, thread list, harness/profile picker, streaming render, tool-call inline rendering, Stop button (AC7), cumulative cost header (AC5/AC8). Real-time updates via subscribeChatMessages helper. Roughly parallel in size to the workers detail page.
  5. Auth posture middleware (AC9) — even as a "single-operator localhost / tsnet for v1" stance, the design note must commit to a posture and a route-gate stub must exist so future auth is schema-local. The bead description flags this as load-bearing for the "remote ops" framing.
  6. Design note in docs/helix/02-design/ answering the six subsidiary questions (persistence scope, harness selection, cost accountability, tool-execution scope, concurrency, auth). The bead's AC1 makes this a hard prerequisite — the implementation PR is supposed to *link* to a landed design note. The note must precede or accompany the implementation; it cannot be a postscript.
  7. Playwright e2e (AC10) — five-step scenario including two browser contexts on the same thread, cancel mid-flight, streamed chunk ordering. Mocking the GraphQL subscription side of this is non-trivial; the existing e2e suite mocks queries via page.route but does not exercise graphql-ws subscriptions.

A single execute-bead commit covering all of the above would either (i) be partial across most of those surfaces and red, or (ii) take many hours of focused work and produce a sprawling diff that the review gate cannot meaningfully verify in one pass. Both outcomes are worse than a clean decomposition.

Additional concrete unknowns the design note must resolve before code can land cleanly:
- Is the chatStream subscription chunk-level (assistant text deltas) or event-level (one event per tool call + one final assistant message)? This determines whether service.Execute needs a new streaming surface or can be wrapped post-hoc.
- For multi-operator on the same thread (AC6), is the second operator a read-only observer or an equal participant who can also send? The bead defaults to "two browsers on the same thread see each other within 2s" which implies multi-participant — but the cost-accountability and session-attribution model needs a per-message author identity that today's auth posture (single-operator) does not surface.
- Does chatSend run in the project worktree or a per-thread worktree? Bead suggests "same as ddx agent run" as the default, but if two threads chatSend concurrently they will race the worktree. This argues for per-thread worktree, which is a real cost (disk, fs setup) and belongs in the design note.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need

Recommend splitting this bead into the following sub-beads, executed in order:

  Sub-bead 1: Design note (docs/helix/02-design/chat-interface.md)
    - Picks Option A vs B vs C vs D and locks it.
    - Answers the six subsidiary questions.
    - Specifies the message/thread schema (manifest.json shape, messages.jsonl entry shape).
    - Specifies whether chatStream is chunk-level or event-level.
    - Specifies worktree scoping per thread.
    - Specifies auth posture for v1 and the gap to broader deployment.
    Size: 1 pass, design only. No code.

  Sub-bead 2: Chat thread storage package (cli/internal/chat/)
    - New Go package: ChatThread, ChatMessage, AppendMessage, LoadThread, ListThreads.
    - Append-only messages.jsonl invariants, atomic manifest writes.
    - Unit tests including concurrent append safety.
    Size: 1 pass.

  Sub-bead 3: GraphQL surface (schema + resolvers + integration tests)
    - schema.graphql edits, gqlgen regen, four resolver files, integration tests.
    - chatSend dispatches via service.Execute and writes the assistant turn as a single message once the session completes (event-level streaming, simplest first cut). Chunk-level streaming is a follow-up.
    - chatStream subscription emits message-created events.
    - Session linkage via SessionIndexEntry.id reference on each assistant message.
    Size: 1 pass.

  Sub-bead 4: Frontend chat pane
    - New ChatPane.svelte component, thread list, message list, input box, harness/profile picker.
    - Mounted on project home page below the actions section.
    - subscribeChatMessages helper in src/lib/gql/subscriptions.ts.
    - Cost rollup in thread header. Stop button calls workerStop on the in-flight session's worker.
    Size: 1 pass.

  Sub-bead 5: Playwright e2e + auth posture middleware
    - Two-context test, cancel test, streaming chunk test (with mocked graphql-ws server).
    - Auth middleware gate stub on chat routes.
    Size: 1 pass.

The bead's AC11 (depends-on ddx-2513f08c, ddx-2ceb02fa) was not verified in this pass. Sub-bead 1 should re-verify those deps before locking the design.

Recommendation: re-queue this bead as a tracking parent and create the five sub-beads above. The current bead description is unusually thorough and most of its content can be lifted verbatim into the design-note sub-bead's body.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-1be8df2b/events.jsonl:5:{"actor":"erik","body":"no_changes
rationale: status: blocked
reason: pre-existing test failures in cli/internal/agent block the lefthook pre-commit go-test gate (AC #5)

WHAT IS DONE (in worktree, uncommitted)
- Renamed TestWorkerHeartbeat_MirrorsPeriodicLivenessWithoutTrackerHeartbeatSpam → TestWorkLoop_MirrorsPeriodicLivenessWithoutTrackerHeartbeatSpam in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_liveness_test.go (AC #1 exact name match).
- Added TestWorkerStatusSidecar_RecordsCurrentBeadAttemptAndLastActivity in the same file (AC #2 exact name match). The test drives a multi-tick ExecuteBeadWorker.Run via runBlockedHeartbeatLoopWithRuntime and asserts the .ddx/workers/<worker-id>/status.json sidecar contains worker_id, current_bead, attempt_id (provisional), phase, last_activity_at, and pid.
- AC #3 (atomic sidecar writes) is already satisfied by workerstatus.WriteLiveness's tmp-then-rename in cli/internal/workerstatus/liveness.go:52-85.
- AC #4 narrow verification command (`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestWorkLoop_MirrorsPeriodicLivenessWithoutTrackerHeartbeatSpam|TestWorkerStatusSidecar_RecordsCurrentBeadAttemptAndLastActivity" -count=1`) is GREEN against the worktree edits.

WHAT BLOCKS
- AC #5 requires `lefthook run pre-commit passes`. Lefthook's go-test hook runs `go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` for any staged file under cli/internal/agent/. That package has TWO pre-existing failures reproducible on the baseline checkpoint (commit 9b6331815) WITHOUT my changes:
  1. TestReadinessUnavailableOutputIsActionable (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go:1479) — actionable readiness warning no longer surfaces in the work log
  2. TestLifecycleHooks_UnpinnedWorkersStillUseProfileSelection (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook_test.go:338) — pre-dispatch lint hook sends empty Policy instead of "cheap"
- Both relate to routing/policy/readiness behaviour (see recent commits 6bd016d7c, 021c9ade4, 8cfb7ab36, 8ea44d438, 3ac1bb88d, 3568e17b0). Out of scope for a sidecar-naming bead and would require its own bead with routing/policy investigation.

WHAT A FOLLOW-UP NEEDS
- Fix or quarantine the two failing tests above so `go test -short ./internal/agent` is green; once that lands, re-attempt this bead and the existing worktree edits commit cleanly.
- Alternatively, file a sibling bead targeting only those two tests, then dispatch this bead after that one merges.

rationale: Bead: B22d-h — delete RunViaService + RunViaServiceWith + runFixtureHarnessViaRunner

STATUS: Cannot be completed in this pass. The bead's premise is false: it claims
"After steps b, c, d all migrated their callers, this deletion is mechanical."
That is incorrect — steps a, e, f, and g (which migrate the remaining callers)
are NOT yet closed.

(a) WHAT IS DONE
Nothing. No code changes were made. Verification only.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING

`ddx bead list | grep B22d` shows the current decomposition state:

  ddx-636ca6d4  closed        B22d-b
  ddx-06267d44  closed        B22d-c
  ddx-c168f77f  closed        B22d-d
  ddx-a4c8ed23  in_progress   B22d-a (grade.go r.Run -> r.runInternal)
  ddx-7c6664be  in_progress   B22d-e (BuildArgs signature off RunOptions)
  ddx-9f4b1a8c  open          B22d-f (SessionIndexEntryFromResult off RunOptions)
  ddx-ce7a796e  open          B22d-g (RunFunc + runCompareArmWith + public
                                       Run*With signatures off RunOptions)
  ddx-b602ccce  in_progress   B22d-h (this bead)

`grep -rn "RunViaService\|RunViaServiceWith\|runFixtureHarnessViaRunner"
cli/internal/agent cli/cmd` shows live, non-test callers that still depend on
the three functions this bead asks me to delete:

  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:214  RunCompareViaService -> RunViaService
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:223  RunCompareWithAgent  -> RunViaServiceWith
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:435  RunQuorumWithConfigViaService -> RunViaService
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:481  RunQuorumViaService  -> RunViaService
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:489  RunQuorumWithAgent   -> RunViaServiceWith
  - cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go:79          dispatchViaResolvedConfig -> RunViaServiceWith
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go:112  RunViaServiceWith
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go:283            RunViaServiceWith

The compare_adapter.go callers are owned by step B22d-g ("RunFunc +
runCompareArmWith + public Run*With signatures off RunOptions"). The two test
files are part of the broader RunOptions retirement (steps e/f/g). Step c's
own commit message (96b243d1) confirms this scope split: "step g/h covers
RunOptions retirement", which means g must run before h is mechanical.

The dispatch.go call site at line 79 (introduced in B22d-d) was deliberately
left calling RunViaServiceWith because RunWithConfigViaService delegates back
through RunViaService — there is no in-package internal helper today that
takes (svc, projectRoot, opts). Inlining the three functions into
RunWithConfigViaService alone (a public ResolvedConfig+runtime function) does
not give dispatch.go a substitute either, because dispatch.go already has
override-resolved opts and would have to build a synthetic ResolvedConfig.

If I tried to satisfy this bead in isolation today I would have to either:
  1. Delete the three functions and watch `go build ./...` fail on at least
     8 call sites — violating "do not commit red code".
  2. Migrate compare_adapter.go (step g), dispatch.go, and the two test files
     myself — silently absorbing scope from B22d-a, -e, -f, -g, which the
     decomposition deliberately separated.
  3. Convert the three deletions into shims (re-export under new names) —
     which contradicts the AC ("removed from cli/internal/agent/service_run.go").

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT NEEDS

Close (or merge into this bead) B22d-a, B22d-e, B22d-f, and especially
B22d-g first. Once B22d-g lands, compare_adapter.go's Run*ViaService and
Run*WithAgent helpers will no longer call RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith,
and the two test files will have been converted off raw RunOptions. At that
point the only remaining caller will be dispatch.go:79, and the deletion
truly is mechanical: rename RunViaServiceWith to a lowercase
runViaServiceWith (or fold it into dispatchViaResolvedConfig), then delete
RunViaService and runFixtureHarnessViaRunner.

Re-queue this bead after B22d-a/e/f/g close. A stronger model is not the
issue — sequencing is.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-b602ccce/events.jsonl:6:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes
rationale: Bead: B22d-h — delete RunViaService + RunViaServiceWith + runFixtureHarnessViaRunner

STATUS: Cannot be completed in this pass. Same blocker as the previous attempt:
the bead's premise — "After steps b, c, d all migrated their callers, this
deletion is mechanical" — is still false. Steps B22d-f and B22d-g, which own
the remaining live callers and the RunOptions retirement, are NOT yet closed.

(a) WHAT IS DONE
Nothing. No code changes. Verification only.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING

Current B22d decomposition state (`ddx bead list | grep B22d`):

  ddx-a4c8ed23  closed         B22d-a
  ddx-636ca6d4  closed         B22d-b
  ddx-06267d44  closed         B22d-c
  ddx-c168f77f  closed         B22d-d
  ddx-7c6664be  closed         B22d-e
  ddx-9f4b1a8c  in_progress    B22d-f (SessionIndexEntryFromResult off RunOptions)
  ddx-ce7a796e  open           B22d-g (RunFunc + runCompareArmWith + public
                                       Run*With signatures off RunOptions)
  ddx-b602ccce  in_progress    B22d-h (this bead)

`grep -rn "RunViaService\|RunViaServiceWith\|runFixtureHarnessViaRunner"
cli/internal/agent cli/cmd` shows live, non-test callers that still depend on
the three functions this bead asks to delete:

  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:214  RunCompareViaService -> RunViaService
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:223  RunCompareWithAgent  -> RunViaServiceWith
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:435  RunQuorumWithConfigViaService -> RunViaService
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:481  RunQuorumViaService  -> RunViaService
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:489  RunQuorumWithAgent   -> RunViaServiceWith
  - cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go:79          dispatchViaResolvedConfig -> RunViaServiceWith
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go:112  RunViaServiceWith
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go:283            RunViaServiceWith

The compare_adapter.go callers are owned by step B22d-g. The dispatch.go call
site is internal plumbing that has no in-package replacement until B22d-g
introduces a Run*With signature that does not take RunOptions, or until a new
internal helper is introduced as part of g/h. The two test files are part of
the broader RunOptions retirement.

Inlining the three functions into RunWithConfigViaService alone (a public
ResolvedConfig+runtime function) does not give compare_adapter.go or
dispatch.go a substitute, because those callers do not have a ResolvedConfig
on hand — they hold override-resolved RunOptions assembled from per-arm
overrides (compare/quorum) or from runtime+overrides (dispatch).

The AC also requires `cd cli && go test ./...` to pass, which is impossible
while session_index_test.go and prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go still call
RunViaServiceWith. Those test sites are owned by step B22d-f/g (test
migration is explicitly out of scope here).

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED

Either:

  1. Close B22d-f and B22d-g first (the proper sequencing the bead description
     itself implies). Once compare_adapter.go and the two oversize/session
     tests are migrated off RunOptions, this bead becomes a mechanical delete
     of three functions plus a single inlining into RunWithConfigViaService.

  2. Or, expand this bead's scope to include the work currently scoped to
     B22d-f and B22d-g — i.e., introduce a service-level internal helper that
     dispatch.go and compare_adapter.go can call without RunOptions, migrate
     all five compare/quorum sites and dispatchViaResolvedConfig onto it,
     migrate the two test sites, and only then delete the three functions.
     This is the "expand scope" path called out in the SD-024 tracker note
     (97561a6e: "atomic cross-caller migration"); it is well beyond the
     "mechanical deletion" scope described in this bead's own description.

The previous attempt at this bead reached the same conclusion. Re-queueing
without first closing B22d-f and B22d-g will reproduce the same blocker.

<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
